low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall be able to define quality checking rules that are specific to environmental situations.
The Clarus system shall be able to store each situation-specific quality checking rule in a persistent configuration accessible by rule identifier.
The Clarus system shall be able to apply a defined situation-specific quality checking rule to an observation when the observation's environmental context matches the rule's situation criteria.
The Clarus system shall be able to generate a quality assessment for an observation based on the applied situation-specific rule without modifying the original observation data.
The Clarus system shall be able to allow a user to modify or delete a situation-specific quality checking rule without affecting other rules.
The Clarus system shall be able to validate that a situation-specific quality checking rule is syntactically correct before storing or applying it.
The Clarus system shall be able to log which situation-specific quality checking rule was applied to each observation, including the rule identifier and the detected environmental situation.

a collected by the Clarus system will include atmospheric data, pavement data , and hydrologic (water level) data.
The Clarus Initiative consists of two development components. 
•	The first component is the development of the Clarus system – a network for sharing, quality controlling, and exchanging surface environmental data and relevant surface transportation conditions. 
•	The second component is the development of tools (such as decision support systems) that make effective use of the Clarus system. 
The addition of Clarus system data to national weather observations will further enhance general purpose weather forecasting, providing a wider range of benefits to the protection of life an

to collect, control the quality of, and consolidate surface transportation environmental data. The Clarus Initiative will address the necessary infrastructure to consolidate the data from a multitude of independent data collection systems. This process offers the prospect of enhancing data coverage, improving the performance of meteorological support services, and providing guidance to owners of these data sources regarding the quality of their data and performance of their data collection systems.
Clarus represents the next step in bringing together surface transportation best practices and the greater weather community. Surface transportation environmental data collected by the Clarus syst

ogical investigators and other researchers. This phase of development does not include directly archiving the large volume of environmental data in Clarus. Considering the technical scope of such an effort, archiving may be externalized or be deferred until the Clarus network is operational and proven.
2.2	System Functions 
The Clarus system will collect data from contributing members, organize and qualify the data, and then publish the data for use by service providers and other members of the network. High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall record statistics about its operation.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall capture quantitative metrics of its operational activities (including data collection events, processing errors, and average processing time) in a system log.
The Clarus system shall persist all captured operational metrics in non-volatile storage to survive system restarts.
The Clarus system shall associate each operational metric entry with a UTC timestamp.
The Clarus system shall ensure that recorded operational metrics remain immutable and are not altered after creation.
The Clarus system shall provide an interface to retrieve recorded operational metrics for inspection and verification.

1.2	Scope 
Clarus is an initiative sponsored by the Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) to organize and make more effective environmental and road condition observation capabilities in support of four primary motivations.
1)	Provide a North American resource to collect, quality control, and make available surface transportation weather and road condition observations so that State Departments of Transportation (DOTs) can be more productive in maintaining safety and mobility on all roads.
2)	Surface transportation-based weather observations will enhance and extend the existing weather data sources that support

High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from (roadway) vehicles.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall provide a network interface to receive environmental data messages from roadway vehicles.
The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from roadway vehicles that includes atmospheric, pavement, and hydrologic measurements.
The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from roadway vehicles in a format compliant with the ConOps specification for roadway vehicle data exchange.
The Clarus system shall assign a unique submission identifier to each environmental data message accepted from roadway vehicles.
The Clarus system shall store accepted environmental data from roadway vehicles in a persistent data store without alteration.
The Clarus system shall generate a receipt acknowledgment for each environmental data submission accepted from roadway vehicles.

High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from transit vehicles.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall accept environmental data messages from transit vehicles via the NTCIP Environmental Sensor Station (ESS) interface.
The Clarus system shall validate that each environmental data message from a transit vehicle contains the required data elements as specified in Table 1 (atmospheric, pavement, hydrologic).
The Clarus system shall log the receipt timestamp and source identifier for each environmental data message received from a transit vehicle.
The Clarus system shall store environmental data received from transit vehicles in the Qualified Environmental Data repository with associated metadata.
The Clarus system shall apply continuous quality control processes to environmental data received from transit vehicles.
The Clarus system shall generate a data quality flag for any environmental data from transit vehicles that fails validation or is out of range.
The Clarus system shall notify the responsible data collector of any data quality condition affecting environmental data from transit vehicles.
The Clarus system shall accept environmental data from transit vehicles of type bus and watercraft (ferry) as defined in ConOps §2.5.5.

The Clarus Initiative consists of two development components. 
•	The first component is the development of the Clarus system – a network for sharing, quality controlling, and exchanging surface environmental data and relevant surface transportation conditions. 
•	The second component is the development of tools (such as decision support systems) that make effective use of the Clarus system. 
The addition of Clarus system data to national weather observations will further enhance general purpose weather forecasting, providing a wider range of benefits to the protection of life an

collect information and disseminate it internally without direction or dependence on Clarus.
Each participating weather network’s connection to Clarus will add two new possible modes of functionality. First, the participant will be able to share collected environmental data with Clarus. Second, participants will be able to receive environmental data collected by Clarus. The primary recipients of this data will be weather service providers, but any Clarus participants would be able to receive data if they so chose. This concept of autonomous data sharing is comparable to the World Wide Web layer of the Internet, where organizations can publish information on web pages, or browse and download

to collect, control the quality of, and consolidate surface transportation environmental data. The Clarus Initiative will address the necessary infrastructure to consolidate the data from a multitude of independent data collection systems. This process offers the prospect of enhancing data coverage, improving the performance of meteorological support services, and providing guidance to owners of these data sources regarding the quality of their data and performance of their data collection systems.
Clarus represents the next step in bringing together surface transportation best practices and the greater weather community. Surface transportation environmental data collected by the Clarus syst

Hardware, operating system, and development environment have significant impacts on the inherent reliability of the system. The primary challenge here will be the trade-off between the desire for reliability and the cost of redundancies.

High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall maintain a dynamic library of data for at least seven days.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall store all received environmental data in a dynamic library.
The Clarus system shall retain each data item in the dynamic library for a minimum of seven days from its receipt timestamp.
The Clarus system shall not delete or remove data from the dynamic library before the seven‑day retention period has elapsed.
The Clarus system shall provide a retrieval function to obtain data from the dynamic library for any time within the seven‑day retention window.
The Clarus system shall continuously update the dynamic library with newly received environmental data.
The Clarus system shall ensure the integrity of data items stored in the dynamic library throughout the retention period.

Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall receive a change notification request.
The Clarus system shall validate the change notification request for required fields and authorized source.
The Clarus system shall identify the data set(s) that have changed as specified in the notification.
The Clarus system shall retrieve the current version of each identified data set from the data store.
The Clarus system shall disseminate the retrieved data to all subscribed recipients using standard Internet protocols.
The Clarus system shall confirm successful delivery of the disseminated data and record the dissemination event.

The Clarus system will collect data from contributing members, organize and qualify the data, and then publish the data for use by service providers and other members of the network. Each participating weather network’s connection to Clarus will add two new possible modes of functionality. First, the participant will be able to share collected environmental data with Clarus. Second, participants will be able to receive environmental data collected by Clarus. The primary recipients of this data will be weather service providers, but any Clarus participants would be able to receive data if they so chose. --- New input ---
High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall allow users to create a data subscription request.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The system shall provide a 'Create Subscription Request' action button on the user dashboard that is accessible to authenticated users.
The system shall display a subscription request form containing fields for selected data set, temporal range start and end, spatial filter, quality criteria flags, and preferred delivery method.
The system shall require the user to be authenticated before allowing access to the subscription request form.
The system shall verify that the authenticated user possesses the 'SubscriptionCreator' permission.
The system shall validate that all required fields in the subscription request form are provided and conform to the defined data types.
The system shall persist the submitted subscription request in the subscription repository with a generated unique subscription identifier.
The system shall return a success response including the generated subscription identifier and a summary of the submitted parameters.
The system shall make the persisted subscription request retrievable by the user for viewing and modification.
The system shall apply quality criteria from the subscription request to filter candidate data sets during processing.
The system shall schedule delivery of subscribed data according to the user‑specified delivery method and frequency.

Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall acknowledge receipt of each new environmental data item.
The Clarus system shall capture and store the exact timestamp of receipt for each new environmental data item.
The Clarus system shall make each new environmental data item available in the published data feed no later than twenty minutes after its captured receipt timestamp.
The Clarus system shall measure and log the elapsed time between the receipt timestamp and the completion of publication for each new data item.
The Clarus system shall ensure that the measured elapsed time for publication does not exceed twenty minutes for any new environmental data item.

--- New input ---
High-level requirement: The Clarus system shall detect out of range values.
Output:

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall receive environmental data from all supported data sources and pass each observation to the out-of-range detection process.
The Clarus system shall maintain a configurable set of out-of-range thresholds for each environmental parameter.
The Clarus system shall compare each observation value against the applicable out-of-range threshold for its parameter.
The Clarus system shall generate an out-of-range quality flag when an observation value exceeds its parameter's threshold.
The Clarus system shall associate the out-of-range flag with the original observation without modifying the observation data.
The Clarus system shall record each out-of-range detection event, including timestamp, source, parameter, value, and threshold, in an audit log.
The Clarus system shall provide out-of-range flags to authorized consumers via standard interfaces.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Clarus system shall provide a capability to initiate a spatial quality check test for a defined geographic area.
The Clarus system shall retrieve environmental data for the defined geographic area from the available data sources to support the spatial quality check test.
The Clarus system shall apply spatial quality check tests to the retrieved data and generate quality flags for data points that do not meet spatial criteria.
The Clarus system shall record the spatial quality check test parameters, applied methods, and resulting flags in a persistent log.
The Clarus system shall allow configuration of which spatial quality check tests are enabled and their parameters.
The Clarus system shall handle cases where insufficient data is available for a spatial quality check by returning a status indicating data insufficiency.
The Clarus system shall provide an interface for users to request and receive spatial quality check results for specified regions and time periods.
